titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Eliot Weinberger Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 3, 2022 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This collection consists of writings, correspondence, printed material, video cassettes, audiocassettes, compact discs, digital files, and other papers by or relating to Eliot Weinberger. The writings document Weinberger's work as an essayist and literary critic, his work as translating authors including Octavio Paz, Beidao, Jorge Luis Borges, and Vicente Huidobro, and his work as an editor of literary anthologies such as the New Directions Anthology of Classical Chinese Poetry (New Directions, New York, 2003). Correspondence includes extensive literary correspondence with poets and authors including Susan Howe, Clayton Eshleman, C.D. Wright, Forrest Gander, Rachel Blau DuPlessis, Marjorie Welish, George and Mary Oppen, and others. Correspondence also documents Weinberger's work on the literary magazine Montemora, his longstanding relationship with the publisher New Directions, and with other magazines, publishers, and organizations. Printed ephemera largely consists of promotional materials for readings, talks, conferences, festivals, and other programs involving Weinberger or authors with whom he worked. Audiovisual materials document performances or appearances by Weinberger or others. The bulk of the digital files consists of email, writings, and research materials.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 266-267 (audiocassettes and videocassettes): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 268-269 (born digital): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Eliot Weinberger Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heris and assigns. Fur further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund and the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2021.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Eliot Weinberger Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: Eliot Weinberger
bioghist.p: Eliot Weinberger (born 1949) is an American essayist, translator, and editor. Weinberger co-founded the literary magazine Montemora (1975-1982), and served as the primary literary translator for Octavio Paz and Beidao, in addition to translating work by Jorge Luis Borges, Vicente Huidobro, and others. Weinberger's writings span political commentary and literary criticism, and appear in books (many published by New Directions), the London Review of Books, the New York Review of Books, and elsewhere. He has edited a number of anthologies, including the New Directions Anthology of Classical Chinese Poetry (New Directions, New York, 2003).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into five groupings: I. Writings, circa 1950-2020. II. Correspondence, 1956-2020. III. Printed ephemera, 1960-2020. IV. Audiovisual materials, 1989-2006. V. Computer media, 2001-2007.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Writings by Weinberger, 1962 - 2020; Translations, circa 1969-2020; and Anthologies, circa 1950-2006.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings document Weinberger's work as an essayist and literary critic, his work as translating authors including Octavio Paz, Beidao, Jorge Luis Borges, and Vicente Huidobro, and his work as an editor of literary anthologies such as the New Directions Anthology of Classical Chinese Poetry (New Directions, New York, 2003).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Alphabetical correspondence, 1956-2020; Montemora correspondence, 1975-1990s; New Directions correspondence, 1976–2019; and Business correspondence, 1970-2020.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence includes extensive literary correspondence with poets and authors including Susan Howe, Clayton Eshleman, C.D. Wright, Forrest Gander, Rachel Blau DuPlessis, Marjorie Welish, George and Mary Oppen, and others. Correspondence also documents Weinberger's work on the literary magazine Montemora, his longstanding relationship with the publisher New Directions, and with other magazines, publishers, and organizations.

arrangement.head: Arrangement

scopecontent.p: Includes itineraries for events and festivals, including a packet for a U.S. Embassy translation event, schedule for a visit of Octavio Paz to the University of Chicago, and other printed materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes group photograph for Banff 2012 with Eliot Weinberger. Select festivals include: The Geraldine R. Dodge Poetry Festival, Internationales literature festival in Berlin, the Mexican Literature Festival at the Poetry Center for the 92nd Street Y, Literaturfest de Stadt in Frankfurt, Del Corazon, Mexican Performing Arts Festival in Chicago, International Literature Festival in Odessa, Hay Festival at the Banff Centre, Festival Internacional Cervantino, PEN World Voices, New Literature from Europe Festival, NGC Bocas Lit Festival in Trinidad, International Poetry Nights in Hong Kong, International Poets' Festival Jerusalem, Feria Internacional del Libro, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Wild City: The 2nd Annual Terra Nova Gathering, The Genesis of the Text: Translating Latin American Literature, Latin American Poetry and Oral Transitions at the University of California in Santa Barbara, Forum on Jorge Luis Borge at the Spanish Cultural Center of New York, Discovering America: The Texts at Barnard for the Eight International Conference on Translation, Kentucky Foreign Language Conference, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes literary awards, special events, calendars of events, and other invitations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains flyers, invitations, program, and other printed ephemera including celebrations of his work, memorials, tributes, and joint appearances.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains flyers, invitations, programs, small posters, and other printed ephemera including poetry readings, lectures, and talks. Includes programs and printed material for Words Without Borders Galas, the National Book Awards, events by New Directions, the International Voices of Poetry Series at Georgetown, Middlebury College's honorary degree ceremony, talks at the New York Public Library and the 92nd Street Y, Poet's House, The Academy of American Poets, The Poetry Project, the Poetry Center at San Francisco State University, and poster for Cecilia Vicuña even with Eliot Weinberger.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes printed material regarding the stage production, including ticket stubs, flyers, programs, press material, newspapers, and cover proofs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 23 badges for festivals, conferences, talks, and for a Nobel conference.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proof covers of Paul Auster's "Translations" and Eliot Weinberger's "What I Heard About Iraq."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes awards from the National Book Circle Critics award and from the Modern Language Association of America.
